claim: JCVI-Syn3A (CP016816.2) encodes 496 genes total: 458 protein-coding (CDS), 29 tRNA, 6 rRNA, 2 ncRNA, 1 tmRNA.
source_detail: Counted `gene` and child feature types in CP016816.2 (file SHA-256 f1d1d19b3624c8391deff73ab60627478df89cd55dfafdfb67d0df14a7680fdf). The 458 CDS count resolves the 452-vs-493-vs-496 dispute tracked in facts/uncertainty/syn3a_gene_count_dispute.json: 452 is Breuer 2019's metabolic-model coverage (subset of CDS), 493 is total genes minus a few classifications we now see as CDS, 496 is the true annotated gene feature count in CP016816.2.
caveats: Counts are of `gene` features in CP016816.2; definitions that count only protein-coding CDS give 458, not 496.
Breuer 2019 reports coverage of 452 protein-coding genes - this is a subset of the 458 CDS (some CDS have no metabolic function and are not in the FBA model).
